name: Glarus (Switzerland)
description: A square red field bearing the figure of Saint Fridolin as a pilgrim in a black habit, walking toward the hoist, with a gold staff, book and halo.
history: The banner of Saint Fridolin, patron of Glarus, goes back to the Battle of Näfels (1388); the present black-robed design was standardised in 1959.

features.role: Saint Fridolin as a pilgrim, habited black, with gold staff, book and halo
features.symbolism: Saint Fridolin, patron saint of Glarus
